pr.title: >50% fuzzy match on all remaining item TUs
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Large item decompilation sweep aimed at bringing the remaining low-fuzzy-match item translation units above roughly 50%. The author explicitly described the work as Claude-assisted but heavily human-cleaned. It filled many item functions and callbacks, refined item variable/attribute structs, and adjusted a few fighter/item call sites. decomp.dev reported matched code rising to 63.04% (+0.35%, +13,772 bytes), matched data to 38.13% (+328 bytes), with 54 new matches and 47 unmatched-item improvements.
what_changed: Filled or improved many previously stubbed item functions across 38 files, especially Sheik needle thrown, Mr. Saturn/Dosei, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Pikachu Thunder Jolt air, Kirby cutter beam, Great Fox laser, Unknown, Kyasarin, Klap, Patapata, Hitodeman, ZGShell, CLink milk, and Climbers string.

Updated shared item headers with more precise item variable and attribute layouts: examples include itCLinkMilk_ItemVars, itClimbersString_ItemVars, itKirby2F23_ItemVars, itHitodemanAttributes, itPikachutJoltAir_ItemVars, expanded itSeakNeedleThrown_ItemVars, itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs, itGreatFoxLaser_Attrs, and refinements to Kyasarin, Dosei, Unknown, and GreatFoxLaser fields.
Changed Sheik needle thrown logic table entries in it_2725.c from it_803F6F50 to it_803F6F50.states, matching the expected state-table member access.
Simplified it_80279C48 from taking Item_GObj* plus Article* to taking only Item_GObj*, then updated Pokémon-style callers such as Chicorita and Entei to use item->xC4_article_data directly.

Adjusted fighter-side call sites to match new item signatures/types: ftSk_SpecialN.c no longer assigns the return value of it_802AFEA8, and ft_0C31.c compares it_802EAF28(...) to 0U rather than NULL after the GreatFoxLaser return type became scalar.

Implemented CLink milk and Climbers string behavior using typed per-item variables rather than unrelated union fields, including spawn setup, parent tracking, accessory callbacks, matrix/JObj handling, and cleanup paths.

decomp.dev examples of new 100% matches included it_2725_Logic7_ShieldBounced, it_802B3F88, it_2725_Logic109_Reflected, it_2725_Logic109_ShieldBounced, itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Coll, it_802D4564, itPatapata_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802C3950, it_802ECD3C, it_802C8B28, and it_8029A218.
smart_moves: Treated the work as a broad fuzzy-match triage pass: many small item callbacks moved from 0% to exact or near-exact matches, producing measurable progress even where full TU matching was not guaranteed.
Used header/struct layout improvements as an enabler for source matching instead of leaving functions full of raw offsets or borrowed union fields.

Preserved common item-spawn idioms repeatedly: initialize SpawnItem, copy prev_pos/pos, zero velocities, set parent_gobj fields, set x44_flag.b0/x40, call Item_80268B18, then initialize item vars and link to owners/bones with helpers such as Item_8026AB54.
Kept exact type details where they affected codegen, such as void-return it_802AFEA8, scalar-return it_802EAF28, state-table .states access, and Item_GObj/Fighter_GObj/HSD_GObj distinctions.
Introduced small static inline helpers in Dosei for repeated JObj rotation/speed patterns, which made the generated code more manageable while still matching low-level HSD behavior.
Cleaned AI-generated output through review-driven include fixes and const/data-placement cleanup rather than merging raw Claude output as-is.
decomp_lessons: AI can accelerate broad item decomp sweeps, but this PR is explicit evidence that human cleanup remained necessary; the author said Claude did not do a great job and that the human was still in the loop.
For item work, accurate xDD4_itemVar union members and attribute structs are often prerequisites for matching many callbacks in the same TU.

Do not leave borrowed item-var fields in place when a real per-item struct can be added; this PR replaced examples like gamewatchchef/bombhei-style accesses in CLink milk and Climbers string with clinkmilk/climbersstring fields.
Offset-commented structs with explicit pads, Vec3 fields, and scalar type corrections can unlock both readability and matching, but semantics should remain conservative when names are still unknown.
Exact return type and comparison form matter: pointer-style NULL comparisons can be wrong when the callee actually returns an integer-like value, as shown by the it_802EAF28/0U change.
Project include style is part of review quality for decomp PRs; MSL/baselib headers should be included explicitly and consistently, not via ambiguous quoted math.h or quoted baselib paths.
The PR title goal should be treated cautiously: the author wrote they were not sure everything actually crossed the 50% fuzzy-match line.
naming_conventions: Existing item callback naming was preserved: numeric symbols like it_802B3F88 and behavior callbacks like itDosei_UnkMotion0_Anim or itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion2_Coll stayed in project style.
Logic callback names follow the existing it_2725_LogicNN_Event pattern, e.g. it_2725_Logic109_Reflected, it_2725_Logic109_ShieldBounced, it_2725_Logic7_Reflected, and it_2725_Logic27_Reflected.
Per-item xDD4 structs use names such as itCLinkMilk_ItemVars, itClimbersString_ItemVars, itKirby2F23_ItemVars, and itPikachutJoltAir_ItemVars.

Attribute structs use conservative item-specific names such as itHitodemanAttributes, itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs, itGreatFoxLaser_Attrs, and itUnknownAttributes when field semantics are still mostly x-offsets.
Struct field comments retained ip+DD4-relative offsets and xNN names, with pads such as _pad, _pad2, pad_0, and x40_pad used when layout is known but semantics are not.
Reviewed include convention favored angle-bracket library includes such as <baselib/gobj.h>, <baselib/jobj.h>, <MSL/math.h>, and <MSL/trigf.h> over quoted library/math includes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and small temporary pads in several filled functions to preserve stack layout for matching.
Used MSL math/trig headers and functions such as atan2f plus M_PI_2/trigf-related includes where item rotation calculations needed matching codegen.
Matched HSD_JObj manipulation directly: HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, HSD_JObjSetScale, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, PSMTXIdentity, and PSMTXConcat patterns appear in the added item code.
Relied on exact item state/motion helper calls such as Item_80268E5C, Item_802694CC, Item_8026BB20, Item_8026BD6C, it_8026B894, and collision callbacks to mirror item lifecycle control flow.

Preserved exact scalar/pointer access width where needed; GreatFoxLaser changed x38 layout to s16 fields while it_802EAF28 returned an s32-like value, with a code comment noting the x38 access-width issue.

Switched it_80279C48 to compute direction from GET_ITEM(item_gobj), item->pos, item->owner, ftLib_8008627C, and ftLib_80086644, removing an unused Article parameter that did not participate in the matched logic.
review_feedback: PsiLupan requested gobj includes use library angle-bracket style rather than quoted baselib paths in itdosei.c and itkirby_2F23.c; the comment text wrote <baseline/gobj.h>, while the final diff shows <baselib/gobj.h>.

PsiLupan challenged leaving it_804DC878 as a TODO for .sdata2 placement: 'Is there a reason not to do it now?'; the final diff shows const f32 it_804DC878 = 0.0f without the TODO from the reviewed hunk.
PsiLupan requested explicit <MSL/math.h> for itlipstickspore.c instead of "math.h".
PsiLupan requested explicit <MSL/math.h> for itpikachutjoltair.c instead of generic <math.h>.
PsiLupan applied the same math-include style feedback to itseakneedlethrown.c and itunknown.c.
The visible review feedback was mostly hygiene around include style and data-placement cleanup, not objections to the broad matching approach.
follow_up_queries: Verify which remaining item TUs, if any, were still below the 50% fuzzy-match target after merge; the author explicitly expressed uncertainty.
Revisit near-matches from the bot report, especially Dosei, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Unknown, GreatFoxLaser, Seak needle thrown collision callbacks, and Pikachu TJolt reflected logic.
Audit any remaining item files for ambiguous math includes such as "math.h" or generic <math.h> where reviewer style would prefer explicit <MSL/math.h>.
Validate newly added ItemVars and Attributes layouts as more item semantics become known; many fields are still xNN/pad placeholders.
Investigate the GreatFoxLaser x38 access-width note in it_802EAF28 and whether a cleaner struct/union representation can replace the likelike-field workaround.
Search for other item xDD4_itemVar accesses still borrowing unrelated union members after this sweep.
